method: larql: `larql bench --cpu`; llama.cpp: ollama gemma3:4b via /api/generate options.num_gpu=0,num_thread=8 (ollama IS llama.cpp, same Q4_K_M GGUF). Homebrew llama-bench could not load the ollama blob.
discrepancy_vs_recorded_C10: ROADMAP.md C10 records 1.50x (27.6 vs 41.4 tok/s, 2026-05-16, direct StandardEngine vs llama.cpp). This re-measure is 1.93x (23.6 vs 45.5) -- WORSE. Reconcile before trusting either: candidates = thermal, short-prompt/small-n, ollama-num_gpu=0 vs direct llama.cpp -ngl 0, or a real decode regression since May. Re-run with a longer multi-prompt corpus on a cool machine.
short_term_tier: Target = within 10% of llama.cpp -ngl 0. NOT met (1.5-1.9x slower depending on measurement).

decode_stage_split_ms_per_tok.shares: FFN ~50% / attn ~38% / lm_head ~12% (raw timer sums slightly >100%: thread-local timers overlap rayon work)

bottleneck: The existing C12 (hand-asm Q4K x Q8K kernel). VERIFIED from source: the production CPU decode path is `attention_decode_step_native` (larql-inference/src/vindex/kquant_forward/cached.rs:563), which quantizes the activation to Q8_K (ll.609/710) and runs Q/K/V/O through the int8 Q8_K SDOT kernel via `matvec_q4k_or_q6k_q8k` -- SAME kernel the FFN uses. So BOTH attention and FFN are already on int8 SDOT; the gap is that larql's SDOT kernel is Rust intrinsics lowered by LLVM vs llama.cpp's hand-written aarch64 asm (two-super-block interleaving + prefetch), ~1.73x per-core, ~63 vs ~95 GB/s effective. Decode is matvec-dominated (FFN ~50% + attn ~38% + lm_head ~12%, all the same kernel).
CORRECTION: An earlier draft of this file claimed 'attention runs f32, wire it to the int8 kernel (new item C11)'. THAT WAS WRONG -- it came from reading larql-compute/src/attention/decode.rs, which is the f32 BLAS / LARQL_Q4K_DIRECT_ATTN opt-in path, NOT the production native decode path. There is no attention-wiring win; attention already uses int8 SDOT. The real lever is C12 (hand-asm kernel). C11 in ROADMAP.md already means 'architecture-rule CI' (unrelated).
fix: C12 as already specced: hand-asm aarch64 Q4K x Q8K matvec on the FFN tile shapes (gate/up/down) + attention projections (same kernel), two-super-block interleaving + prefetch to match llama.cpp. Acceptance: >=9.5 tok/s single-core, >=39 tok/s 8-thread on Gemma 3 4B Q4K. Spec: crates/larql-compute/docs/q4k-decode-kernel.md.
caveats: Small n (both early-stopped on a short prompt). lm_head CPU matvec path not separately traced. The 1.93x-vs-1.50x discrepancy is unresolved.
notes: 26B-A4B llama.cpp baseline (the medium-term pin) needs a 26B GGUF -- not local.
